MyGRACE-PhasePlan.NAME: Encoding and Access Hardening
MyGRACE-PhasePlan.Goal: Eliminate UTF-8 mojibake in user-facing delivery surfaces and make MTProto access depend on issued per-SNI credentials rather than the shared base secret.

Steps.step-1: Define explicit UTF-8-safe source, build, payload, and `/verify` rendering expectations for all Russian user-facing copy.
Steps.step-2: Add a private per-SNI secret salt to the runtime configuration contract and propagate it into the unified Erlang release boot path.
Steps.step-3: Replace raw-secret client credential assembly with derived per-SNI fake-TLS secrets and define idempotent reissue behavior for existing users.
Steps.step-4: Align domain whitelist policy application with per-SNI secret enforcement so only issued domains with derived credentials can authenticate.
Steps.step-5: Define migration, regression, and rollout gates including forced reissue of previously delivered links after per-SNI secret rollout.

ExitCriteria.Item: Verification email subject, HTML, text, and `/verify` result pages render readable UTF-8 Russian copy end-to-end.
Client access using raw `PROXY_SECRET_HEX` is rejected once hardening is enabled; only issued derived credentials succeed.
Operator documentation and verification gates explicitly cover salt handling and reissuing all previously issued fake-TLS links.
